title: Amylotheca boholensis
intro: Amylotheca boholensis, also known as the Philippine herbal tea tree or Bohol tea tree, is a plant species native to the Philippines that has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Amylotheca boholensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to promote digestion, boost the immune system, and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Amylotheca boholensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is native to the Philippines and is also known as the Philippine corkwood tree. The plant is a member of the genus Amylotheca, which consists of a limited number of species. Amylotheca boholensis is further classified as a succulent tree with unique characteristics and adaptations. Its classification within the plant kingdom is that of a dicotyledonous angiosperm.
uses: Amylotheca boholensis is a plant species native to the Philippines, and it has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
